Documentation for adding HTTP method overrides in Flask using middleware.

# Purpose
The content describes a method to handle HTTP method overrides using middleware in a Flask application. Some HTTP proxies do not support certain HTTP methods, such as `PATCH`. To address this, the client can send an HTTP `POST` request with the `X-HTTP-Method-Override` header specifying the desired method. The `HTTPMethodOverrideMiddleware` class intercepts requests, checks if the method in the header is allowed, and modifies the request method accordingly before passing it to Flask. This middleware is integrated into a Flask application by wrapping the `app.wsgi_app` with `HTTPMethodOverrideMiddleware`.
